title: [NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD] 28kwh Voltx Home Solar Battery $3,499 Installed ($3299 with Referral discount) @ Voltx Energy
body:
Deal: [NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD] 28kwh Voltx Home Solar Battery $3,499 Installed ($3299 with Referral discount) @ Voltx Energy

[63pinecone] Submitted the form when the last deal was posted and I never heard back…
[cooldude123] Had a very frustrating experience with VoltX: I paid a deposit and was promised an installation for over 4 months and kept stalling saying that they were imminently installing
They ultimately cancelled days before the May 1 deadline - this deal might work out if you get an immediate installation but otherwise consider finding other quotes at the same time
[parkmichael] Very similar experience here. After dozens of phone calls (almost all weren’t answered) and 2 cancelled install dates I requested a refund. There isn’t a deal good enough to make me deal with this company again.
[custom] Same experience in Adelaide. Waited 8 months after continually being told it would be installed, installers in the area etc. useless
[samehada] It was hectic in April when installer jacked up installation charge and some battery brands also jacked up price.
Companies that still has installation slot at that time also increase price. So if you have missed it before 1/5 then that is the unfortunate.
Many was getting Voltx 50kw battery + 10kw inverter for $5000 in NSW installed in April.
[custom] Signing up in August 2025, and to be not installed by 1st April is inexcusable. Particularly when each phonecall advised 6 weeks time
[jackary] @samehada: Mate do you work for Voltx? Trying to justify what the average punter would agree is unjustifiable is strange.
[samehada] @jackary: I don't work for them, I don't need to justify for them either just stating the obvious fact at that time. Is it so hard for you to swallow the fact?
There are so many ppl got their system installed from Voltx from previous $3999 deal posted on ozbargain before 1/5.
If they can't do it since August 2025 the he should have looked at other options. The lowest price was promoted during March 2026.
[aleayr] @samehada: Is it possible to revoke a positive vote with down votes like a negative vote? This feels like straight out astroturfing.
[samehada] @aleayr: Mate, you can vote whatever you like but it will need to be related to the deal as per vote guidelines or it will be revoked.
[aleayr] @samehada: You may not work for them, but can you state your association with VoltX?
Have you had a system installed by them? Are you friends or otherwise know someone who works for them?
Your posting behaviour is odd for someone with no skin in the game.
[samehada] @aleayr: I don't need to justify anything to you.
[Mikael] @aleayr: @aleayr Not a big world. Just to look at older posts seeing associated with sunline that's been posted in the past.
[aleayr] @Mikael: Ah good call, so definitely not impartial.
[qwerty40777] Anyone got this battery installed from a previous deal? How was the installation experience and app?
[eddyah] Did you see the posts created 4 minutes before yours?
JFC.
[tunzafun001] Neighbor got the final 50kw $3k deal, plus panels.
Sub contractor literally dragged the panels across his tin roof one handed (smoking a dart with the other)..slid em over gutter. So his tin roof is f'd. Panels probably stress cracked.
Then drilled straight through his roof, through the eave below. Then ran a conduit straight across the roof to the second array.
Left packaging in the gutter..
Never seen anything like it.
Grabbed a business card to make sure I never get them.
P. S . He is / was on the 48c premium FIT. Installer said he won't lose it with this battery….
[garygaz] I had a work colleague get a 50kw battery off these guys about 4 months ago - he said the experience was all good. Came when they said they would and had been working fine with the app since. Just to balance out the stories.
[atlas] Could something like this be AC coupled to an existing Sigenergy battery/inverter?
[samehada] You won't get rebate again if you had already claimed it for your Sigenergy battery.
[Mkak] Had mine installed. Took a few months but no dramas with anything else.
[Mr Snuffleupagus] Don’t hold your breath… Still no response from them from previous ‘deal’.
